4488	  share the same underlying carrier. Indeed, keeping the \(\mathcal{F}\)-side open in its
4489	  iterated-image form \(W = \iota(V)\) (rather than rewriting it to a basic open), the carriers coincide
4490	  definitionally by the restriction-of-objects identity of Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}; the only
4491	  difference between the two sides is the bundled module packaging --- an \(R_g\)-module on the left versus
4492	  an \(R\)-module on the right --- which lives at the level of the ambient module category, not in the
4493	  carriers.
4494	
4495	  The scalar action on each side is likewise definitional. By
4496	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq} the native \(R\)-action of the global-ring section functor
4497	  \(\Gamma_R(-,\mathcal{F})\) is the structure-sheaf action of the restricted global-sections image; by
4498	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modulesRestrictBasicOpen_smul_eq} the \(R_g\)-action of the tile
4499	  \(\Gamma_{R_g}(-,\mathcal{F}_{(g)})\) transports through the two open-immersion ring isomorphisms to the
4500	  same ambient \(\mathcal{F}\)-action on the same carrier. Thus the carrier identification of
4501	  Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib} is already additive and respects restriction maps and naturality in
4502	  \(V\); to make it the asserted \(R_g\)-linear isomorphism it remains only to reconcile the two scalar
4503	  prescriptions.
4504	
4505	  Comparing the two action formulas (and reducing to the case \(V = \top\), so that \(W = D(g)\) by
4506	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}), the sole remaining content is a single structure-sheaf ring
4507	  identity: for \(r \in R\),
4508	  \[
4509	    \rho^{D(g)}\bigl(\theta_R(r)\bigr)
4510	      \;=\;
4511	    \beta_g^{-1}\bigl(\theta_{R_g}(\bar r)\bigr),
4512	    \qquad \bar r \;=\; \text{image of } r \text{ in } R_g,
4513	  \]
4514	  where \(\theta_R : R \xrightarrow{\sim} \Gamma(\Spec R,\mathcal{O})\) and \(\theta_{R_g} : R_g
4515	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \Gamma(\Spec R_g,\mathcal{O})\) are the global-sections identifications,
4516	  \(\rho^{D(g)}\) is restriction of sections to \(D(g)\), and \(\beta_g\) is the ring isomorphism on global
4517	  sections induced by the affine identification \(\Spec R_g \cong D(g)\). This identity is exactly the
4518	  statement that \emph{the sections of \(\mathcal{O}\) over \(D(g)\) are the localisation \(R_g\),
4519	  compatibly with the algebra map \(R \to R_g\)}: the left-hand side restricts \(r\) to \(D(g)\), and the
4520	  right-hand side is the same element viewed through the affine identification of \(D(g)\) with
4521	  \(\Spec R_g\) applied to the localisation map.
4522	
4523	  There are two routes to this ring identity.
4524	  \emph{(A) Geometric.} The open inclusion \(D(g) \hookrightarrow \Spec R\) induces the identity on
4525	  sections, so the composite of the two open-immersion ring isomorphisms is the global-sections map of the
4526	  localisation morphism \(\Spec R_g \to \Spec R\), which is \(\Spec\) of the algebra map \(R \to R_g\).
4527	  The identity then follows from the naturality of the \(\Spec\)-to-global-sections comparison
4528	  (\(\Gamma\)-\(\Spec\) naturality) applied to that algebra map.
4529	  \emph{(B) Algebraic.} Rewriting \(W = D(g)\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}, the ring
4530	  \(\Gamma(D(g),\mathcal{O})\) is the localisation of \(R\) away from \(g\); both sides of the displayed
4531	  identity are then \(R\)-algebra maps \(R \to R_g\) into this localisation, and they agree by uniqueness
4532	  of the localisation comparison map.
4533	  The residual is a single focused structure-sheaf sub-proof; the carrier and scalar bookkeeping above is
4534	  definitional.
4535	\end{proof}
4536	
4537	\begin{lemma}[Per-tile section localisation at \(f\)]
4538	  \label{lem:tile_section_localization}
4539	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tile_section_localization}
4540	  \uses{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover, lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen,
4541	    lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib,
4542	    lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap,
4543	    lem:tile_image_opens_identities, lem:tile_section_comparison}
4544	  Let \(X = \Spec R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, let \(f \in R\),
4545	  and let \(g \in R\) be either a cover element \(g = g_j\) or an overlap product \(g = g_j g_k\) of the
4546	  finite presentation cover of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}, so that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) of
4547	  \(\mathcal{F}\) to \(\Spec R_g\) admits a global presentation. Then the restriction-of-sections map
4548	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) exhibits its target as the localisation of
4549	  its source at the powers of \(f\); equivalently, there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
4550	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\).
4551	\end{lemma}
4552	\begin{proof}
4553	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen, lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation,
4554	    lem:restrict_obj_mathlib, lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap,
4555	    lem:tile_image_opens_identities, lem:tile_section_comparison}
4556	  % NOTE: the naive recipe "the section comparison is the restrict_obj rfl" is UNSOUND:
4557	  % restrict_obj is rfl only for the local-ring SheafOfModules functor Γ(M,-), whereas
4558	  % the localisation in this file lives in the global-ring functor (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj),
4559	  % which does NOT commute with restriction definitionally. Hence the five-step descent below.
4560	  Let \(\iota : \Spec R_g \xrightarrow{\sim} D(g) \hookrightarrow X\) be the open immersion of
4561	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}, with \(\bar f\) the image of \(f\) under the
4562	  localisation map \(R \to R_g\). The proof is a base-ring descent in five steps; the section
4563	  comparison it rests on is \emph{not} the definitional equality of
4564	  Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib} (see the proof of
4565	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_section_comparison}).
4566	
4567	  \emph{Step 1 (global presentation of the tile).} Since \(D(g)\) lies in a presentation member of the
4568	  cover, step B4 (Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen}) presents the tile
4569	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) globally over \(\Spec R_g\).
4570	
4571	  \emph{Step 2 (localisation over \(R_g\)).} Apply
4572	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation} to the globally presented
4573	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) and \(\bar f\): the section-restriction map
4574	  \(\Gamma_{R_g}(\Spec R_g, \mathcal{F}_{(g)}) \to \Gamma_{R_g}(D(\bar f), \mathcal{F}_{(g)})\)
4575	  exhibits its target as a localisation at the powers of \(\bar f\), \emph{as \(R_g\)-modules}. Note this
4576	  is over \(R_g\), not yet over \(R\).
4577	
4578	  \emph{Step 3 (opens identities).} By Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities} the two image opens of
4579	  \(\iota\) are \(\iota(\Spec R_g) = D(g)\) and \(\iota(D(\bar f)) = D(gf) = D(g) \cap D(f)\).
4580	
4581	  \emph{Step 4 (the section comparison).} By Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_section_comparison} there is an
4582	  \(R_g\)-linear isomorphism, natural in \(V\) and intertwining restriction maps,
4583	  \(\Gamma_{R_g}(V, \mathcal{F}_{(g)}) \cong \Gamma_R(\iota(V), \mathcal{F})\). This is the load-bearing
4584	  step: it bridges the global-ring section functor (where Step 2's localisation lives) and the ambient
4585	  sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(R\); it is a genuine natural-isomorphism construction, not a definitional
4586	  identification. Transporting Step 2's localisation-at-\(\bar f\) property across this isomorphism
4587	  (\(R_g\)-linearly) and matching source and target opens by Step 3 yields: the map
4588	  \(\Gamma_R(D(g), \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma_R(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) exhibits its target as a
4589	  localisation at the powers of \(\bar f\), \emph{still as \(R_g\)-modules}.
4590	
4591	  \emph{Step 5 (base-ring descent to \(R\)).} Finally descend the base ring from \(R_g\) to \(R\) by
4592	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap}, applied with \(A = R_g\) and the
4593	  scalar tower \(R \to R_g\) on the two section modules: since \(\bar f\) is the image of \(f\) under
4594	  \(R \to R_g\), the powers of \(\bar f\) descend to the powers of \(f\), and the \(R\)-linear
4595	  restriction of the Step-4 map exhibits \(\Gamma_R(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) as the localisation of
4596	  \(\Gamma_R(D(g), \mathcal{F})\) at the powers of \(f\) \emph{over \(R\)}. Equivalently there is a
4597	  canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\).
4598	
4599	  Crucially this localisation is carried out entirely on the tile \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\), where
4600	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is associated to a module (the tile is globally presented, hence of the form
4601	  \(\widetilde{M_g}\)), and never on
4602	  the global object \(\mathcal{F}\). This is exactly the structural feature that keeps the keystone
4603	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule} non-circular: the keystone-at-\(g\) statement is
4604	  \emph{not} invoked; only the presented-tile lemma is.
4605	\end{proof}
4606	
4607	\begin{lemma}[Kernel comparison: the localised global equalizer matches the \(D(f)\)-cover equalizer]
4608	  \label{lem:qcoh_section_kernel_comparison}
4609	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_section_kernel_comparison}
4610	  \uses{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover, lem:qcoh_section_equalizer,
4611	    lem:localized_module_map_exact_mathlib, lem:tile_section_localization}
4612	  Let \(X = \Spec R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, let \(f \in R\),
4613	  and let \(g_1, \ldots, g_n\) be the finite presentation cover of
4614	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}, with \(\operatorname{span}\{g_j\} = R\). Then the
4615	  canonical \(R\)-linear map \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) induced by
4616	  \(\rho_f : \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) (the localisation lift of \(\rho_f\)
4617	  at the powers of \(f\)) is an isomorphism.
4618	\end{lemma}

36	### Decision: D1 — the GLUE step (algebraic span-cover descent vs sheaf-axiom equalizer)

52	  progress. `h j` is **un-dischargeable** from the 5 DONE pieces alone.
53	- **Mathlib idiom (the non-circular glue)**: the equalizer / sheaf axiom, NOT the algebraic local-global

62	  "sections-localise" inputs sit on the tiles (where `F` is tilde), and the global statement is recovered

64	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong. `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` is the wrong glue for this goal.
65	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — supply the sheaf-axiom equalizer gluing (the keystone itself

93	## On bridge.md's B6 over-claim
94	B6 ("section comparison is `restrict_obj`-rfl", Sheaf.lean:328) **conflated two different comparisons**.
95	`restrict_obj : Γ(M.restrict ι, U) = Γ(M, ι ''ᵁ U)` is rfl and identifies the *tile sheaf's* sections
96	with `F`'s sections over the image open — a **geometric=geometric** identity. But the span-cover descent's
97	actual per-`j` obligation is the **abstract↔geometric** identity
98	`LocalizedModule (powers gⱼ) Γ(X,F) ≅ Γ(D(gⱼ),F)`, which is the keystone, NOT rfl. B6 used the (true) rfl
99	of the first to claim the descent's section comparison is free; the descent in fact needs the second.
100	The planner's suspicion is correct — B6 over-claimed.
101	
102	## Recommendation
103	**Do NOT close the keystone via `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` on global sections — that route is
104	circular.** Re-shape the glue to the sheaf-axiom equalizer (Stacks 01HV(4)):
105	
106	1. **[reuse, DONE]** B1 → finite cover `{gⱼ}`, `span = ⊤`; B4 → each tile `F_{(gⱼ)}` is presented;
107	   tile lemma → per cover/overlap element, `Γ(D(gᵢ…),F)_f ≅ Γ(D(gᵢ…·f),F)` (`section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation`,
108	   read via `restrict_obj`).
109	2. **[new, small]** the 2-term sheaf-axiom equalizer `0→Γ(X,F)→∏Γ(D(gᵢ),F)→∏Γ(D(gᵢgⱼ),F)` as a
110	   `Function.Exact` (the sheaf condition of `(Spec R).Modules`; degree-0/1 of the project's own
111	   `sectionCechComplex`, or Mathlib's sheaf equalizer). Same for the cover of `D(f)`.
112	3. **[new, Mathlib]** localise the X-cover equalizer at `f` with **`IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`**
113	   (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Exact`).
114	4. **[new, bookkeeping]** intertwine the localised equalizer with the `D(f)`-cover equalizer via the
115	   per-tile isos of step 1 (commutativity = naturality, the analogue of `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison`);
116	   the kernel comparison yields `Γ(X,F)_f ≅ Γ(D(f),F)` = the keystone.
117	
118	The project's P3 section-Čech bridge (`sectionCech_objD_apply`, `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison`, the
119	`phi`/`phiL` ladder, `sectionToModuleAddEquiv`) is the **template** for step 4, but it is currently wired

121	0/1** of a **general qcoh `F` whose tiles are tilde**. Build a degree-0/1 specialisation, not a full reuse.
122	Estimate: ~150–300 LOC, fiddly equalizer/naturality plumbing, **no** mathematical wall (contrast the two
123	walls of Route P). The keystone is the load-bearing gap-fill; everything downstream of it (the Handoff
124	chain in `QcohTildeSections.lean:559-604`) then closes.

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohRestrictBasicOpen
open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
universe u
variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

theorem appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    Scheme.Hom.appTop f ≫ (Scheme.Hom.appIso f ⊤).inv
      = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_top : f ''ᵁ ⊤ ≤ ⊤)).op := by
  rw [Iso.comp_inv_eq, Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom, Scheme.Hom.appTop, ← Category.assoc,
    Scheme.Hom.naturality, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
  trans (Scheme.Hom.app f ⊤ ≫ X.presheaf.map (𝟙 _))
  · rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
  · congr 1

theorem key_morph (g : R) :
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_top : specAwayToSpec g ''ᵁ ⊤ ≤ ⊤)).op
    = CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))
      ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).inv
      ≫ ((specAwayToSpec g).appIso ⊤).inv := by
  have h1 : CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))
        ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).inv
      = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop (specAwayToSpec g) := by
    rw [specAwayToSpec_eq]; exact Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality _
  rw [reassoc_of% h1, appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res]

theorem hBI_lemma (g : R) :
    (Scheme.Hom.appIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ⊤).inv
      ≫ (Scheme.Hom.appIso (specBasicOpen g).ι ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ ⊤)).inv
    = ((specAwayToSpec g).appIso ⊤).inv
        ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (eqToHom (Scheme.Hom.comp_image
            (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv (specBasicOpen g).ι ⊤).symm).op := by
  have hc := Scheme.Hom.comp_appIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv (specBasicOpen g).ι ⊤
  rw [Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso] at hc
  rw [hc]
  simp [Iso.trans_inv, eqToHom_map, eqToHom_op]

theorem MI (g : R) :
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_top :
        ((specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
          (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens))) ≤ ⊤)).op
    = CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))
      ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).inv
      ≫ (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens) ≤ ⊤)).op
      ≫ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv.appIso ⊤).inv
      ≫ ((specBasicOpen g).ι.appIso ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ ⊤)).inv := by
  rw [show (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens) ≤ ⊤)).op = 𝟙 _ from by
      rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens) ≤ ⊤))
        (𝟙 ⊤)]; simp, Category.id_comp]
  rw [hBI_lemma, ← reassoc_of% key_morph]
  congr 1
  rw [← Functor.map_comp]
  congr 1
#print axioms MI

exact ⟨p, hf, rfl⟩

/-- For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, post-composing the global-sections map `f.appTop` with the
inverse of the section iso `f.appIso ⊤` recovers the structure-sheaf restriction from `⊤` to the
image open `f ''ᵁ ⊤`.  Project-local: the section-restriction reading of the open-immersion `appIso`,
the geometric brick of the structure-sheaf ring identity inside `tile_scalar_compat`. -/
theorem appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    Scheme.Hom.appTop f ≫ (Scheme.Hom.appIso f ⊤).inv
      = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_top : f ''ᵁ ⊤ ≤ ⊤)).op := by
  rw [Iso.comp_inv_eq, Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom, Scheme.Hom.appTop, ← Category.assoc,
    Scheme.Hom.naturality, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
  trans (Scheme.Hom.app f ⊤ ≫ X.presheaf.map (𝟙 _))
  · rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
  · congr 1

/-- **`ΓSpec` naturality of `specAwayToSpec g`, section form.**  Restricting the global-sections
identification along `D(g) ↪ Spec R` equals the localization map `R → R_g` followed by the
global-sections identification of `Spec R_g` and the (inverse) section iso of
`ι = specAwayToSpec g = Spec.map (algebraMap R R_g)`.  Project-local: the structure-sheaf naturality
that powers `tile_scalar_compat`; route (A) of the blueprint sketch. -/
theorem key_morph (g : R) :
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv
      ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_top : specAwayToSpec g ''ᵁ ⊤ ≤ ⊤)).op
    = CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))
      ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).inv
      ≫ ((specAwayToSpec g).appIso ⊤).inv := by
  have h1 : CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))
        ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).inv
      = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop (specAwayToSpec g) := by
    rw [specAwayToSpec_eq]; exact Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality _
  rw [reassoc_of% h1, appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res]

/-- The two (inverse) open-immersion section isos of the tile identification compose, via
`comp_appIso`, into the single section iso of `ι = specAwayToSpec g`, up to the structure-sheaf
transport along the image-opens identity `ι ''ᵁ ⊤ = (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ (iso ''ᵁ ⊤)`.
Project-local: the `comp_appIso` bookkeeping consumed by `tile_scalar_compat`. -/
theorem tile_appIso_comp (g : R) :
    (Scheme.Hom.appIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ⊤).inv
      ≫ (Scheme.Hom.appIso (specBasicOpen g).ι ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ ⊤)).inv
    = ((specAwayToSpec g).appIso ⊤).inv
        ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (eqToHom (Scheme.Hom.comp_image
            (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv (specBasicOpen g).ι ⊤).symm).op := by
  have hc := Scheme.Hom.comp_appIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv (specBasicOpen g).ι ⊤
  rw [Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso] at hc
  rw [hc]; simp [Iso.trans_inv, eqToHom_map, eqToHom_op]

/-- **The structure-sheaf ring identity of Sub-lemma B (morphism form).**  Combining `key_morph`
(`ΓSpec` naturality) and `tile_appIso_comp` (`comp_appIso` bookkeeping): the restriction to the tile
image open `D(g)` of the `Spec R` global-sections identification equals the localization map `R → R_g`
followed by the `Spec R_g` global-sections identification and the two (inverse) open-immersion section
isos of the tile.  Project-local: the morphism-level content closed elementwise in
`tile_scalar_compat`. -/
theorem tile_section_ring_identity (g : R) :
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_top :
        ((specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
          (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens))) ≤ ⊤)).op
    = CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))
      ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).inv
      ≫ (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens) ≤ ⊤)).op
      ≫ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv.appIso ⊤).inv
      ≫ ((specBasicOpen g).ι.appIso ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ ⊤)).inv := by
  rw [show (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens) ≤ ⊤)).op = 𝟙 _ from by
      rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens) ≤ ⊤))
        (𝟙 ⊤)]; simp, Category.id_comp]
  have hr := reassoc_of% key_morph (R := R) g
  rw [tile_appIso_comp, ← hr, ← Functor.map_comp]

set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
/-- **Sub-lemma B scalar compatibility (Stacks 01I8).**  For a quasi-coherent `F` on `Spec R` and
`g r : R`, the native `R`-action of `r` on a section of `F` over the tile image open `D(g)`
coincides with the `R_g`-action of `algebraMap R R_g r` on the corresponding section of the affine
tile `modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F`.  This is the load-bearing scalar reconciliation of the tile
section comparison: the two `rfl` bridges (`modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq`,
`modulesRestrictBasicOpen_smul_eq`) reduce both actions to a structure-sheaf scalar action, and the
remaining ring identity is `tile_section_ring_identity`.  Project-local; consumed by
`tile_section_localization`. -/
lemma tile_scalar_compat (F : (Spec R).Modules) (g r : R)
    (x : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F)).presheaf.obj
          (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens))) :
    (r • (show (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj
            (Opposite.op ((specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
              (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)))) from x))
      = (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) r) • x := by
  rw [modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq F]
  rw [show (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) r) • x
        = _ from modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F) ⊤
            (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) r) x]
  rw [modulesRestrictBasicOpen_smul_eq]
  congr 1
  have hG := congrArg (fun m : CommRingCat.of (R : Type _) ⟶ _ => m.hom r)
    (tile_section_ring_identity (R := R) g)
  simp only [CommRingCat.comp_apply] at hG
  convert hG using 2

101:noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpen (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
298:noncomputable def presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen
---
-- deep instance search (sheafification + colimit-preservation over the localized site); the default
-- `synthInstance.maxHeartbeats` is insufficient.
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
open SheafOfModules in
/-- **Route B, step B4.** If `M.over U` carries a presentation and `D(g) ⊆ U`, then the affine
restriction `modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M`, as a `(Spec R_g).Modules`-object, admits a global
presentation. Assembled by transporting the B2 over-presentation across the B3 bridge isomorphism
`overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` and the restriction along the affine identification. -/
noncomputable def presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen
    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens)
    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (g : R) (hg : specBasicOpen g ≤ U) :
    (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M).Presentation := by
  letI P2 : (M.over (specBasicOpen g)).Presentation := presentationOverBasicOpen M U P g hg
  letI P3 : ((modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse.obj
      (M.over (specBasicOpen g))).Presentation :=
    P2.map (modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse (.refl _)
  letI P4 : (M.restrict (specBasicOpen g).ι).Presentation :=
    Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} (overBasicOpenIsoRestrict g M).hom P3
  -- The instance is supplied as `hpc` (and the functor universe pinned to `u`): inline instance
  -- search for `PreservesColimitsOfSize` of the affine restriction does not fire on its own here.
  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
      (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) := inferInstance
  exact @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P4
    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) hpc
    (restrictBasicOpenUnitIso g)

end RestrictOverBridge

end AlgebraicGeometry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections
open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
universe u
variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

-- the tile section restriction map over R_g
example (F : (Spec R).Modules) (g f : R)
    (U : (Spec R).Opens) (P : (F.over U).Presentation) (hg : specBasicOpen g ≤ U) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f))
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F)).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
  section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation _ (presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen F U P g hg) _

-- the F restriction map D(g) → D(gf) (= D(g*f))
example (F : (Spec R).Modules) (g f : R) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g)))
      →ₗ[R] ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g*f)))) :=
  ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
    (homOfLE (by rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul]; exact inf_le_left :
      PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g*f) ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g)).op).hom
EOF

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/QcohTildeSections.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (5, all axiom-clean, kernel-verified):**
  `appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res`, `key_morph`, `tile_appIso_comp`, `tile_section_ring_identity`,
  `tile_scalar_compat` (the iter-044 named target = Sub-lemma B `lem:tile_section_comparison`'s
  residual ring identity).
- **Declarations blocked: 0** for this iter's target. `tile_section_localization` NOT added — it is the
  next downstream assembly (now fully unblocked); precise gap below.
- **Sorry count: 0 → 0** across the file (the `tile_scalar_compat` scaffold I added mid-session
  carried a temporary `sorry`; it is now fully closed — no `sorry` remains anywhere).
- `#print axioms` for all 5 new decls = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (verified via a fresh
  `lake env lean`, not just the LSP — per the kernel-soundness trap warning). `lake build` succeeds.

## tile_scalar_compat (Sub-lemma B residual) — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** The two `rfl` bridges (`modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq`, `modulesRestrictBasicOpen_smul_eq`,
  done iter-043) reduce both scalar actions to a structure-sheaf `Γ(W,𝒪)`-action; `congr 1` leaves a
  single ring identity. That identity is route (A) of the blueprint sketch (ΓSpec naturality), closed
  by the new morphism-level lemma `tile_section_ring_identity` applied elementwise
  (`congrArg (·.hom r)` + `simp [CommRingCat.comp_apply]` + `convert … using 2`).
- **The in-file "PROVEN tactic prefix" comment was VALIDATED** (progress-critic enforcement): `lean_goal`
  confirmed the prefix `rw [modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq F]; rw [show … from …]; rw [modulesRestrictBasicOpen_smul_eq]; congr 1`
  produces exactly one `case e_a` ring-identity goal before I built on it.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

### Supporting lemmas (all RESOLVED, axiom-clean, reusable)
- `appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res {X Y} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : f.appTop ≫ (f.appIso ⊤).inv =
  Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f ''ᵁ ⊤ ≤ ⊤)).op` — general; the section-restriction reading of `appIso`.
  Proof: `Iso.comp_inv_eq`, `appIso_hom`, `Scheme.Hom.naturality`, thin-cat `Subsingleton`/`congr`.
- `key_morph (g : R)` — ΓSpec naturality of `specAwayToSpec g = Spec.map (algebraMap R R_g)`:
  `(ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ ρ^{D(g)} = ofHom(algebraMap R R_g) ≫ (ΓSpecIso R_g).inv ≫ ((specAwayToSpec g).appIso ⊤).inv`.
  Proof: `Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `specAwayToSpec_eq` + `reassoc_of%` + `appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res`.
  **Crucial finding:** must be stated/proved in `Scheme.ΓSpecIso.inv` form (NOT
  `StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso.hom`): the latter's codomain is in `Spec.structureSheaf`-functor form,
  which is defeq-but-not-syntactic to `(Spec R).presheaf`, breaking `Category.assoc`/`rw` composability
  with `appTop`. The `globalSectionsIso ↔ ΓSpecIso.inv` swap is `CommRingCat.hom_ext rfl`.
- `tile_appIso_comp (g : R)` — `comp_appIso` bookkeeping folding the two tile section isos
  `(basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv.appIso` and `(specBasicOpen g).ι.appIso` into the single
  `(specAwayToSpec g).appIso ⊤`, up to a `presheaf.map (eqToHom (comp_image).symm).op` correction.
  Proof: `Scheme.Hom.comp_appIso` + `Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso` (= refl) + `simp [Iso.trans_inv, eqToHom_map, eqToHom_op]`.
- `tile_section_ring_identity (g : R)` — the assembled morphism identity (combines `key_morph` +
  `tile_appIso_comp` + a thin-cat restriction merge `← Functor.map_comp; congr 1`).

## tile_section_localization — NOT ADDED (next, fully unblocked, ~100–150 LOC engineering, no math wall)
- **Approach scouted (all pieces typecheck):** `presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen F U P g hg` (B4) →
  `section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation tile Ptile (algebraMap R R_g f)` gives
  `IsLocalizedModule (powers f̄) m_tile` over `R_g`, where
  `m_tile = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj tile).presheaf.map (homOfLE (D(f̄) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom`. Then
  `isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap` descends to `powers f` over `R`.
- **The genuine remaining obstruction (confirms [[keystone-tile-reconciliation-not-rfl]]):** the source
  carriers are at DIFFERENT bundled module categories — `Γ_{R_g}(⊤,tile) : ModuleCat R_g` vs
  `Γ_R(D(g),F) : ModuleCat R` — so the `rfl` carrier identity FAILS at the `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj`
  (bundled) level (kernel-checked: "Type mismatch ModuleCat R vs ModuleCat R_g"). The defeq the
  iter-043 bridges use lives at the UNDERLYING-type / `F.val.obj` level, NOT the bundled level.
  So the base-ring descent must be applied to the UNDERLYING types, providing:
  (1) a `Module R` instance on the tile's underlying section type (transported from the `F` side via the
      underlying-type defeq), (2) `IsScalarTower R R_g` on it — supplied by `tile_scalar_compat` via
      `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul`, (3) the underlying restriction-map equality
      `m_tile = ρ_W` (likely needs the `restrict`-functoriality map defeq, to verify), (4) an `eqToHom`
      transport of the opens `ι ''ᵁ (inv ''ᵁ ⊤) = D(g)`, `ι ''ᵁ (inv ''ᵁ D(f̄)) = D(gf)`
      (`tile_image_opens_identities`, propositional).
- **Next step (precise):** build the section comparison as a bare-function `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`
  over the underlying type, OR directly `letI` the `Module R` + `IsScalarTower R R_g` instances on the
  tile's underlying section type and feed `m_tile` (R_g-linear) to base-ring descent, then transport the
  opens by `eqToHom`. The blueprint `lem:tile_section_localization` proof (5-step descent) is the recipe.
- **Dead-end warning:** do NOT expect `m_tile.restrictScalars R = ρ` at the BUNDLED `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj`
  level — kernel-confirmed the carriers are not even the same `ModuleCat`. Work at `F.val`/underlying-type
  level.

## Needs blueprint entry
The following NEW non-private declarations have NO blueprint block (they are `lean_aux` until the
planner/reviewer blueprints them). All are in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/QcohTildeSections.lean`:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res` — general open-immersion lemma:
  `f.appTop ≫ (f.appIso ⊤).inv = restriction to f ''ᵁ ⊤`. Relies on: `Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom`,
  `Scheme.Hom.naturality`, thin-cat subsingleton of `(Opens)ᵒᵖ`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.key_morph` — `ΓSpec` naturality of `specAwayToSpec g`. Relies on:
  `Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`, `specAwayToSpec_eq`, `appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.tile_appIso_comp` — `comp_appIso` fold of the two tile section isos. Relies on:
  `Scheme.Hom.comp_appIso`, `Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.tile_section_ring_identity` — assembled morphism identity. Relies on `key_morph`,
  `tile_appIso_comp`, thin-cat `Functor.map_comp`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.tile_scalar_compat` — Sub-lemma B scalar compatibility (= the content of the
  blueprint `lem:tile_section_comparison` reduced to its scalar core). Relies on the two iter-043 `rfl`
  bridges + `tile_section_ring_identity`. **The blueprint `lem:tile_section_comparison` should get its
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tile_scalar_compat}` hint** (it currently has no `\lean{}`); the four helper
  lemmas above want their own small blocks.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** 5 axiom-clean declarations added (file lines ~798–924 after the
  `tile_image_opens_identities` lemma):
  `appTop_appIso_inv_eq_res`, `key_morph`, `tile_appIso_comp`, `tile_section_ring_identity`,
  `tile_scalar_compat`. The last IS the iter-044 named target (Sub-lemma B's single residual structure-
  sheaf ring identity), now CLOSED and kernel-verified axiom-clean. The whole `tile_scalar_compat`
  obstruction the prior 3 iters narrowed (150 LOC → 1 ring identity) is discharged.
- **Did not attempt-and-leave anything in comments:** the route (A)/(B) options the blueprint named were
  attempted — route (A) (ΓSpec naturality) is what landed.
- **Stopped at `tile_section_localization`** not because it is "hard" but because it is a genuinely
  separate ~100–150 LOC assembly whose obstruction I precisely characterized above (bundled-`ModuleCat`
  carrier mismatch ⟹ must descend at the underlying-type level with `letI`'d `Module R` + `IsScalarTower`
  instances + `eqToHom` opens transport). It was explicitly the "assembles once Sub-lemma B lands" item in
  PROGRESS and the next-iter step; attempting it in this session risked breaking a verified-clean file.
  All ingredients are now axiom-clean and present; the path is engineering, not math.
